name: KEPIER PIT
units: fathoms/feet/inches, transcribed verbatim — convert ONLY downstream

annotations: 'Outset' = sinking term for the initial surface excavation / made ground; final letters cramped, best reading

intervals.verbatim: Blue metal, with two 4" seams of coal
annotations: contains two 4-inch coal seams, not separately measured
'whin' = miners' term for very hard rock (commonly hard siliceous sandstone in Durham sections); lithology not further specified
'black stone' = dark carbonaceous shale in Durham usage

underlined sub-heading in original immediately above this interval: 'Bored in 1818:' — strata below were bored (shaft section above was sunk, undated); year reading probable, glyphs favour 1818 over 1858
sum slip: clerk wrote depth 46-1-4; arithmetic gives 46-1-11 (46-0-11 + 0-1-0); next interval's depth 46-3-3 is consistent with 46-1-11, confirming the slip
depth consistent with the corrected 46-1-11 for the previous interval; running total self-heals here
mixed lithology: mudstone with coal, coal not separately measured

intervals.verbatim: COAL, left off in, owing to water
annotations: water observation: boring abandoned in this coal 'owing to water'

notes: Header reads 'SECTION OF Kepier Colliery'; Surface Level 'c. 293'' O.D. with a faint illegible pencil mark beneath (possibly a later conversion). 'Date of boring or sinking' left blank; 'Communicated ... by B&S. 1224' (reading probable — likely a Borings & Sinkings ledger reference; could also be read 'BrS.'). One-inch Map 27, Six-inch Durham 27 NW. Form is a 2/49-printed BGS sheet, i.e. a mid-20th-century transcript of the original colliery record. Shaft section (intervals 1-14) sunk to the Hutton seam, undated; underlined sub-heading 'Bored in 1818:' (year probable, could be 1858) introduces intervals 15-23, a bore continued from the shaft bottom — the top-level year field carries this 1818 date since it is the only date in the record. One clerk sum slip at interval 16 (depth written 46-1-4, arithmetic gives 46-1-11; the running total self-corrects at interval 17); all other 22 depth checks sum cleanly (6 ft/fathom, 12 in/ft). Final depth 53-5-3 = 323.25 ft = 98.53 m, matching the index 98.46 m. No pencil seam-correlation marginalia; pencil '66' beside the stamped sheet number 36 (with a crossed-out '9') is registration numbering. Page 2 is a blank continuation sheet (printed table header only, no strata).
